vBRIEFInfo.description: Adoption blocker: scripts/release.py only checks tag availability at Step 9 of a 12-step pipeline, after Steps 1-8 have already promoted CHANGELOG, rendered ROADMAP, built dist artifacts, and made a release commit locally. A duplicate-tag failure at Step 9 strands the operator with manual recovery via git reset --hard.

plan.title: fix(scripts/release.py): pre-flight should verify release tag is available before state mutation

narratives.Problem: scripts/release.py invokes `git tag v<version>` at Step 9 of a 12-step pipeline. By that point Steps 1-8 have already mutated state: CHANGELOG promoted to `[<version>]`, ROADMAP regenerated, pyproject.toml + uv.lock synced to the new version, dist/deft-<version>.zip built, and a `chore(release): v<version>` commit made locally. A duplicate-tag failure at Step 9 leaves an unpushed wrong-version commit on master with orphaned build artifacts; recovery requires `git reset --hard` (which AGENTS.md SCM rules forbid without explicit permission). Surfaced 2026-05-01 during the v0.23.0 release attempt where the operator typed `0.22.0` (the 12-hour-old prior release) and the pipeline ran 8 steps before failing.
narratives.Overview: Add a new `[X/N] Pre-flight tag availability` step to release.py Phase 1 (pre-flight), placed before any state mutation. Check three surfaces: local tag (`git tag -l`), remote tag (`git ls-remote --tags origin`), and GitHub release (`gh release view --repo`). Read-only; safe on dry-run; produces actionable recovery message naming the conflict source.
narratives.Background: Current Phase 1 pre-flight (release.py): Step 1 working tree clean, Step 2 branch == base, Step 3 vBRIEF lifecycle sync (#734), Step 4 CI (`task ci:local`). None inspect tag availability. The e2e rehearsal (`task release:e2e`, #716) uses tag `v0.0.1` against an auto-created temp repo, so the production tag namespace is never exercised. Sibling precedent: #734 added vBRIEF lifecycle sync as a new pre-flight step that refuses before mutation; this work follows the same pattern.
narratives.Constraint: MUST be Phase 1 (pre-flight) -- before CHANGELOG promotion (current Step 5), build (current Step 7), or commit (current Step 8). MUST be read-only (no `gh api` writes; safe on `--dry-run`). MUST renumber _TOTAL_STEPS and existing pipeline-step assertions (mirrors the 11 -> 12 shift from #734). MUST surface in dry-run output as `DRYRUN (would verify v<version> tag not present locally / remote / GitHub release)`. MUST NOT auto-increment the version on conflict (silent recovery is the wrong shape).
narratives.Risk: Low. Read-only check; the only side effect is the pipeline refusing earlier. Renumbering _TOTAL_STEPS touches existing tests but the pattern is well-established by #734. Edge case: `gh release view` returns non-zero on missing release, which is the OK path -- must not conflate gh-CLI failure with a missing release.
narratives.Outcome: Acceptance: (1) New pre-flight step verifies local tag, remote tag, and GitHub release availability for v<version> before any state mutation. (2) Step lands in Phase 1 (definitely before Step 5 CHANGELOG, Step 7 build, Step 8 commit). (3) _TOTAL_STEPS bumped; existing pipeline-step assertions renumbered. (4) Dry-run reports `[X/N] Pre-flight tag availability... DRYRUN (would verify ...)`. (5) Production reports `OK` on clean state, `FAIL (<reason>)` with actionable recovery on conflict (suggest different version; most likely cause is operator typo). (6) skills/deft-directive-release/SKILL.md Phase 1 gains a `!` rule citing this issue and the v0.22.0 -> v0.23.0 incident. (7) CHANGELOG entry under [Unreleased] / Fixed.
narratives.Test: tests/cli/test_release_tag_availability.py (NEW) covering: clean state passes; local-only tag conflict fails with actionable message; remote-only tag conflict fails; GitHub-release-only conflict fails; combinations; gh-CLI not-found != release-exists. Existing pipeline-step assertions in tests/cli/test_release.py / test_release_skip_flags.py / test_release_summary.py renumbered for the new _TOTAL_STEPS.
narratives.Action: 1. Add `check_tag_available(version, repo) -> Result` helper in scripts/release.py mirroring the issue's reference implementation. 2. Wire into Phase 1 (between Step 2 branch check and Step 3 vBRIEF sync, OR as Step 5 -- placement matters less than 'before mutation'). 3. Bump _TOTAL_STEPS; renumber subsequent step labels. 4. Add tests/cli/test_release_tag_availability.py. 5. Renumber existing pipeline-step assertions across test_release.py / test_release_skip_flags.py / test_release_summary.py. 6. Add `!` rule to skills/deft-directive-release/SKILL.md Phase 1. 7. CHANGELOG [Unreleased] / Fixed entry. 8. task check; commit; push; PR.
narratives.Urgency: Adoption blocker for release operators. Strands the operator with wrong-version local commit + wrong-version build artifact + manual git-reset recovery. v0.23.0 cut hit it once already. Blast radius small (one helper + one wiring point + renumber); fix is mechanical.

items.title: Add check_tag_available(version, repo) helper to scripts/release.py

narrative.Action: Pure-Python stdlib + gh subprocess. Three surfaces: `git tag -l`, `git ls-remote --tags origin refs/tags/v<version>`, `gh release view v<version> --repo <repo> --json tagName`. Returns Result-shaped object (mirror existing pattern in release.py). Dry-run returns DRYRUN status without invoking gh.

items.title: Wire helper into Phase 1 pre-flight; bump _TOTAL_STEPS

narrative.Action: Insert new step before any state mutation (CHANGELOG promotion / build / commit). Bump _TOTAL_STEPS by 1; cascade-renumber subsequent step labels in run_pipeline. Match the pattern established by #734.

Cover: clean state OK; local-only conflict FAIL; remote-only conflict FAIL; gh-release-only conflict FAIL; combinations; gh-CLI not-found is NOT a conflict. Mock subprocess.run + gh helpers; no real network.

items.title: Renumber pipeline-step assertions across existing release tests

narrative.Action: tests/cli/test_release.py, test_release_skip_flags.py, test_release_summary.py -- update _TOTAL_STEPS expected value and any explicit step-label substring assertions. Mirror the 11 -> 12 shift from #734.

items.title: Add `!` rule + recurrence record to deft-directive-release SKILL Phase 1

narrative.Action: Cite this issue and the v0.22.0 -> v0.23.0 incident as recurrence record. Phase 1 gate already lists vBRIEF lifecycle sync (#734) and task ci:local; this slots in alongside.
fix(scripts/release.py): refuse early when target tag exists locally, on origin, or as a published GitHub release (#784) -- prevents wrong-version CHANGELOG promotion + orphaned dist artifacts.

items.title: Run task check and confirm clean

narrative.Action: Pre-PR gate per main.md Decision Making bullet.
